name: Morgan Stanley Private Bank, National Association
description_short: Morgan Stanley Private Bank, National Association offers brokerage accounts, savings accounts, and checking accounts through the E*TRADE platform with $0 commission trading and competitive deposit rates.
description_long:
Morgan Stanley Private Bank, National Association operates as a Member FDIC banking institution providing financial services primarily through the E*TRADE platform. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail banking and investment products designed for individual investors and savers. Originally established as a standalone brokerage, E*TRADE is now integrated within Morgan Stanley's private banking division, combining wealth management capabilities with accessible self-directed investing tools. The institution serves millions of customers seeking commission-free trading and competitive savings rates.

The bank provides three primary product categories: brokerage accounts for stock, ETF, options, and futures trading; savings accounts including a Premium Savings Account with promotional APY rates; and checking accounts with competitive yields. Brokerage services feature $0 commission on stocks, ETFs, and mutual funds, with tiered pricing on options ($0.65 per contract, $0.50 with 30+ trades quarterly) and futures ($1.50 per contract). The Premium Savings Account currently offers a limited-time 4.00% APY for six months (base rate 3.35%), while the Max-Rate Checking Account provides 2.00% APY with a $15 monthly fee waived at $5,000 minimum balance. Account opening is available online, by phone (800-387-2331), or by mail.

Morgan Stanley Private Bank distinguishes itself through aggressive promotional offers, including up to $10,000 cash credits for new retirement accounts and $1,500 on brokerage accounts with qualifying deposits. The institution has earned multiple awards, including recognition as the #1 Web Trading Platform for 14 consecutive years (Stockbrokers.com) and Best Savings Account for Investors three years running (Wall Street Journal's Buy Side). The platform emphasizes transparent, zero-commission pricing with detailed rate sheets and educational resources including live Wednesday webinars.

The primary caveat is that this is a self-directed investing platform rather than a full-service wealth management firm. While promotional rates and cash credits are attractive, customers must meet specific funding deadlines and deposit thresholds ($1,000 minimum for most offers). Savings rates revert to lower base rates after promotional periods. The institution does not appear to offer advisory services, retirement planning, or dedicated wealth managers based on available website content. This is best suited for active traders and self-directed investors comfortable managing their own portfolios. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Futures trading ($1.50 per contract, excluding cryptocurrency)
Premium Savings Account with promotional and base APY rates
pros: $0 commission trading on stocks, ETFs, options, and mutual funds
Limited-time 4.00% APY on Premium Savings Account for six months (base 3.35%)
Up to $10,000 cash credit for new retirement account deposits by 6/30/26
Up to $1,500 cash bonus on new brokerage accounts with qualifying deposits
#1 Web Trading Platform award for 14 consecutive years (Stockbrokers.com)
Multiple account opening methods: online, phone, or mail
FDIC Member Bank status with transparent pricing and published rate sheets
cons: Promotional APY rates expire after 6 months, reverting to significantly lower base rates
Self-directed platform requires active portfolio management with no advisory services mentioned
Promotional cash credits require minimum funding thresholds ($1,000+) within 60-day windows
Monthly checking account fee ($15) unless $5,000 minimum balance is maintained
Options and futures contracts carry per-contract fees despite stock commission elimination
best_for: Active retail traders seeking commission-free stock and options trading
Self-directed DIY investors comfortable managing their own portfolios
Savers looking for competitive APY rates on deposit accounts with FDIC protection
Customers rolling over 401(k)s or opening retirement accounts with promotional incentives
diagnosis: Morgan Stanley Private Bank, National Association is best for self-directed investors and active traders seeking commission-free trading combined with competitive savings products. The main caveat is that this is a transactional platform, not a wealth management or advisory service, and promotional rates are time-limited with lower ongoing base rates.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
